slug: 北欧神话的世界末日_诸神和怪物同归于尽
title: 北欧神话的世界末日——诸神和怪物同归于尽

outline.concept: Ragnarok is not a battle between good and evil where good wins — it's a prophecy the gods already know they'll lose. Odin has seen it; he prepares anyway. The Norse apocalypse is certain, not conditional. No repentance, no last-minute rescue.
outline.query: 明知会输 仍然去做 预感坏结局 必然失败

outline.concept: Ragnarok triggers through a specific irreversible chain: Fimbulwinter (three consecutive winters with no summer), then Loki breaks free, Fenrir's bonds snap, Jormungandr rises from the ocean poisoning the sky. Each link locks in the next — once started, the sequence cannot be halted.
outline.query: 连锁反应 一发不可收拾 domino 崩溃 无法挽回

outline.concept: The deaths come in destined pairs: Odin vs Fenrir, Thor vs Jormungandr, Tyr vs Garm, Heimdall vs Loki. Each god kills their nemesis and dies in the same encounter. Mutual destruction isn't the tragedy — it's the structure.
outline.query: 两败俱伤 同归于尽 双方都倒下 没有赢家

outline.concept: After Surtr's fire consumes everything, a green world rises from the water. Two humans survive hidden in a tree; Baldr walks free from Hel. Destruction is not the endpoint — it's the reset. The cycle begins again with survivors who carry the memory of what was lost.
outline.query: 废墟 新生 结束后重新开始 浴火重生 循环
